title: End-to-end dictionary for one open-access pilot dataset
context: The pilot proves the whole pipeline end-to-end on one open-access dataset selected on a realistic adoption path. Binding guardrail: open-access / aggregate / de-identified data only; controlled-access and identifiable data are EXCLUDED at gate-002; provenance on every assertion; the deliverable is documentation about the data, never the data itself. It must PASS gate-002 with a committed artifact and reach 100% provenance before any output ships.
objective: Produce one complete, source-verified dictionary for an open-access pilot dataset, emitted in all formats and adopted (or submitted with the blocker surfaced).
acceptanceCriteria: Pilot selected on a realistic adoption path first: informal channel or self-serve fallback (GitHub PR to the dataset's repo, or a Zenodo record/DOI).
Dataset PASSed gate-002 (accessTier: open, license permits derivatives, no identifiable data) with the artifact committed.
Complete field dictionary with coded-value meanings + sentinels; 100% provenance coverage; field coverage >= 95% (gaps listed); quality score recorded (after >= 90/100).
Valid JSON + Frictionless + Croissant emitted; validator clean in CI.
Provenance recorded (source URLs, publisher, version, retrieval date, attribution; license snapshot = committed copy + SHA-256 + Wayback URL).
Adopted via informal channel or self-serve fallback with the Steward's outcomes/<dataset-id>.json recorded - or submitted with the blocker surfaced.
output: output/pilot-008/ dictionary (Markdown + JSON + Frictionless + Croissant) plus the committed gate artifact.
